title: leucantha iberica
intro: Leucantha iberica, also known as White Sage, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of White Sage have been linked to various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as having antimicrobial and antiviral effects. White Sage can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
